name: missingno (Missing Data Visualization)

performance.notes: matplotlib-based rendering; proportional to DataFrame size

agent_readiness.notes: Visualizes missing data patterns in pandas DataFrames. import missingno as msno. msno.matrix(df) — sparkline matrix showing nullity patterns by row. msno.bar(df) — bar chart of completeness per column. msno.heatmap(df) — correlation of missingness between columns. msno.dendrogram(df) — hierarchical clustering of missingness correlation. All functions accept pandas DataFrame. figsize param. sort='ascending'/'descending' for ordering. filter parameter for sampling large DataFrames. sparkline=True/False. freq for time-indexed data. Returns matplotlib axis — can be further customized. Works seamlessly in Jupyter.

gotchas: Very focused tool — only does missing data visualization (by design)
Minimal active development — simple enough to be stable
Large DataFrames (>1M rows) need filter parameter for performance
best_when: EDA (exploratory data analysis) to understand missing data patterns
Quick DataFrame completeness overview in data cleaning workflows
Data quality reports in Jupyter notebooks
avoid_when: When a one-line summary is sufficient (df.isnull().sum())
Non-Jupyter reporting environments needing static images
summary: missingno visualizes missing data patterns. msno.matrix(df), msno.bar(df), msno.heatmap(df), msno.dendrogram(df). matplotlib-based. MIT by Aleksey Bilogur. Extremely simple API — one function per visualization type. Best for EDA missing data analysis. Stable, minimal maintenance needed. Large DFs need filter param. No type annotations. Complements pandas isnull() with visual patterns.
